meta.pageTitle: Mai Quoc Viet – Backend Developer | VietDon | Da Nang
meta.description: Mai Quoc Viet (maiviet283) – Backend developer from Da Nang. Building VietDon, an online donation platform for Vietnamese creators. Python, Django, FastAPI, Node.js.

hero.title: I write code with curiosity and live through small journeys.
hero.body: I am Mai Quốc Việt, a young backend developer from Da Nang. I love sunny mornings, small trips close to nature, and the idea of Vietnamese people building thoughtful products for the Vietnamese market. Code is not only work to me; it is a way to turn curiosity, pride, and life energy into something people can actually use.

stats.label: migrated backend from sync to async

family.title: Family
family.body: Some things keep me grounded: family, days spent around trees, and a few short lines that are easy to remember but heavy enough to shape how I live.

api.title: A few living signals pulled straight from public APIs.
api.body: This portfolio is not only static copy. It catches a few real pulses: how GitHub is moving, what Da Nang feels like today, and where Vietnam time is right now.

vietdon.title: VietDon: online donations for Vietnamese creators.
vietdon.body: VietDon comes from a very Vietnamese thought: if viewers want to support the creators they love, the flow should feel fast, familiar, and as natural as opening a banking app. I am building it from the core: creator registration, KYC, public donation pages, VietQR/Pay2s, streamer wallet, revenue dashboard, and realtime notifications.

flow.title: Creator sets up a channel
flow.body: Register, complete KYC, configure the page, and connect a bank account for payouts.

flow.title: Viewer donates fast
flow.body: No complicated login. Choose an amount and pay with VietQR or Pay2s.

flow.title: Wallet updates in realtime
flow.body: Once the payment is confirmed, the dashboard updates and the creator tracks revenue directly.

stack.title: I like building a solid core, then wrapping it in an experience with soul.
stack.beliefBody: A good product needs both strong engineering and a human feeling. Clear API contracts, database indexes that match real flows, cache in the right hot paths, and deployments with logs and monitoring. But in the end, what matters most is whether it helps people do something easier, happier, and lighter.

work.title: I learn from real systems and real people, then bring that back into my own products.

items.body: Currently working in a real product environment, sharpening fullstack skills, system thinking, and the path from feature idea to production.
Built routing modules for a nationwide route-finding system, working with large map datasets using Python, FastAPI, and GeoPandas. Focused on speed, PostgreSQL queries, and async architecture.
Worked close to real infrastructure: routing, security fundamentals, and monitoring with Nagios and ELK Stack. This helped me see backend as a running system, not only code.

projects.title: Projects I use to sharpen product thinking, backend engineering, and systems work.

items.name: Network Intrusion Detection & Alert System
items.detail: A Python + Elasticsearch system that collects and analyzes security logs from Zeek, Nginx, and Django to detect abnormal behavior and send realtime alerts.
features: Detects DDoS, SQL Injection, directory brute-force, and login brute-force patterns
AI Advisor uses ChatGPT API to suggest mitigation actions for each threat
Notifier sends alerts and recommendations to administrators via Telegram

items.name: BookStore
items.detail: Django REST Framework, JWT, MySQL, Redis, React TypeScript
features: RESTful APIs for a book management system
Redis caching to reduce database load

items.name: Clothing Shop API
items.detail: E-commerce backend with auth, anti-spam, Redis cache, Gunicorn, and Nginx
features: Backend API for an e-commerce flow
Security mechanisms: brute-force protection, anti-spam, and request validation
Production deployment with Gunicorn and Nginx
